title: P0: Invitee lands on the project they were invited to
description: When accepting an invitation that has a project_id, redirect to /orat?project=<id> with a welcome toast. Spec: e2e/invitee-lands-on-invited-project.feature

title: P0: Invite collaborator directly to a project (project-scoped role)
description: Migration 020 extends invitations with optional project_id + project_role enum (editor|viewer). Project-level invite form + accept flow grows orat_project_members. Spec: e2e/invite-collaborator-to-project.feature

title: P0: Send invitation emails automatically
description: When orat_create_organization_invitation succeeds, dispatch an email containing the invite link. New lib/email/ module with provider abstraction (default: Resend). No schema change. Spec: e2e/invitation-email-delivery.feature

title: P0: Pending invitations visible & manageable in project Team view
description: ProjectTeamView shows pending invitations alongside members; resend and revoke actions. Spec: e2e/pending-invitations-visible-and-managed.feature

title: P0: Group-by in the List view
description: Add group-by capability to ListView with options: status, assignee, project, due-date bucket, none. Collapsible groups with counts. Persist choice via localStorage. Spec: e2e/group-tasks-in-list.feature

title: P0: Saved & shareable filtered views
description: New table orat_saved_views (migration 019) with RLS. UI: save current filters+grouping as named view, list, share-link, update, delete. Spec: e2e/saved-task-views.feature

title: P0: Task priority (High/Medium/Low)
description: Add priority field to orat_tasks (migration 018), expose in TaskDialog, display on Board/List/Gantt cards, sort by priority on List. Default to Medium. Spec: e2e/task-priority.feature

title: P0: Overdue swimlane on the Board
description: Add an Overdue column to KanbanView so tasks whose effective status is Overdue (past current_due_date and not Complete) remain visible. Currently they vanish from the board because the column array only contains Not Started/In Progress/Complete. Spec: e2e/overdue-tasks-visible-on-board.feature

title: Rewrite e2e/home.spec.ts to assert current copy
description: All 3 home.spec.ts tests assert /construction pm/i but the rendered h1 is 'Owner's Rep Action Tracker' (the brand name 'Construction PM' only lives in metadata.title). Update assertions to match the visible heading. Keep the 3 tests (renders heading + auth links, navigates to login, navigates to signup) — just fix the heading regex.
acceptance_criteria: All 3 home.spec.ts tests pass against the current src/app/page.tsx. Assertions use semantic queries (getByRole heading) and match the rendered text 'Owner's Rep Action Tracker' (use a regex like /owner.?s rep action tracker/i).

title: Delete e2e/dashboard.spec.ts (routes no longer exist)
description: e2e/dashboard.spec.ts visits /dashboard and /tasks. middleware.ts (lines 33-36) redirects both to /orat, which then redirects unauthenticated users to /login. Both tests are guaranteed to fail. Delete the file. A real /orat E2E (with programmatic auth) is tracked separately.
acceptance_criteria: e2e/dashboard.spec.ts is deleted. npm run test:e2e no longer reports its 2 failures.

title: Fix org-data Supabase mock chain (replace with recorder/Proxy fake)
description: Two tests in src/app/orat/lib/org-data.test.ts fail because the hand-rolled Supabase mock terminates at fixed depth: 'returned projects have organizationId set' fails with supabase.from().select().in().order is not a function, and 'getTasksForProject filters by both ids' fails with eq().eq().order().order is not a function. Replace the per-test chainable stubs with a single Proxy-based query-recorder fake that returns itself for chain methods and resolves with the configured {data,error} only when awaited (PromiseLike). Keep existing assertions; add no new behavior.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 existing org-data.test.ts tests pass. The mock returns itself for any chain call (.select/.eq/.in/.order/.single/...) and only terminates when awaited. No more brittle per-test chain depths.

title: Fix Vitest setup: install @testing-library/dom + jest-dom and add vitest.setup.ts
description: The login page test suite fails to load (0 of 2 tests run) because @testing-library/react peer dep @testing-library/dom is not installed. Install @testing-library/dom and @testing-library/jest-dom to devDeps. Create vitest.setup.ts at repo root that imports @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest. Wire it via setupFiles in vitest.config.ts. Verify src/app/(auth)/login/page.test.tsx loads and passes.
acceptance_criteria: npm run test:run loads and passes both tests in src/app/(auth)/login/page.test.tsx (no peer-dep error).

title: Prevent account enumeration on sign up
description: Sign up currently surfaces signUpError.message verbatim, including 'User already registered', which lets anyone probe whether an email has an account. The flow should look identical for new and existing emails (always show the verify-email screen), and existing users should receive an email steering them to log in or reset their password.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/signup-prevent-account-enumeration.feature.

title: Make sign-up password length match server policy (8 chars)
description: Client form enforces minLength=6 with hint text 'At least 6 characters', but Supabase is configured with minimum_password_length = 8. Users typing 6 or 7 characters pass client validation, hit submit, and get a confusing server error. Update the client minimum and hint text to 8 to match server policy.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/signup-password-length-requirements.feature.

title: Add 'Confirm password' field to sign up form
description: Sign-up form has a single password input, so a typo silently locks the user out of an account they just created. Add a Confirm password field with client-side mismatch validation that prevents submission until the two values match.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/signup-confirm-password.feature.

title: Send actual verification email on signup
description: Sign-up currently calls supabase.auth.signUp with emailRedirectTo and routes the user to /signup/verify-email, but supabase/config.toml has [auth.email] enable_confirmations = false, so no email is ever sent and the verify-email page misleads users. Either enable email confirmations (and configure SMTP for prod) so the verify-email page is truthful, or remove the verify-email redirect and log the user in directly. Recommended: enable confirmations to match the existing user story.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior must match e2e/signup-email-verification.feature: a confirmation email is delivered after signup, the user sees the verify-email page, the link in the email logs them in (or routes them to log in).
close_reason: Merged via feat/auth-enable-email-confirmations; supabase/config.toml now enables email confirmations.

title: P1: Real-time board updates
description: Supabase Realtime subscriptions on orat_tasks so two users on the same Board see each other's drag/edit/create live. Includes conflict resolution for sort_order during concurrent drags. Optional: presence avatars.

title: P1: True external collaborator model (no org_members row)
description: Trade-off from P0 orat-pjv: project-scoped invitees are added to organization_members. Replace with project-only access via RLS that admits users in orat_project_members for the owning org's tables.

title: P1: Task comments with @mentions
description: Comment thread on tasks; @mention notifies project members; edit/delete own. Spec: e2e/task-comments-and-mentions.feature

title: P1: Email reminders for assignments and due dates
description: Email on assignment, 3-day pre-due reminder, daily overdue digest, opt-out. Spec: e2e/due-date-email-reminders.feature

title: P1: Clients organize projects
description: Add a Client entity. Projects roll up to a client. Filter dashboard by client. Spec: e2e/clients-organize-projects.feature

title: Expand org-data tests: cover updateProject, updateTask, updateTaskStatus, reorderTasks, deleteTask success + negative paths
description: Once the Supabase mock is replaced with a recorder fake (dep), expand src/app/orat/lib/org-data.test.ts (or add adjacent files) to cover the mutation paths that today have zero tests. For each: assert that the captured organization_id matches the current user's org, the success branch returns {data}, and the cross-org branch returns {error: /not in your organization|denied/i}. Cover: createProjectForOrganization success path (RPC + members insert + externals insert), updateProject (membership reconciliation: insert vs update vs delete diff), updateTask, updateTaskStatus (history append), reorderTasks (the /api/orat/reorder handler too), deleteTask. Reference the actions defined in src/app/orat/actions.ts and lib/org-data.ts to understand the surface. Server actions are where org-scoping is enforced — the mutation paths silently rely on RLS as a backstop today.
acceptance_criteria: Tests for createProjectForOrganization (success), updateProject, updateTask, updateTaskStatus, reorderTasks, and deleteTask exist and exercise both the success and cross-org rejection branches. All pass under the new recorder-fake mock. npm run test:run is green.

title: Add /orat E2E: create project → create task → drag to In Progress → reload
description: Smallest change that turns the E2E suite from 'login form smoke test' into actual product coverage. Using e2e/helpers/auth.ts (see dep), write e2e/orat.spec.ts that: 1) logs in programmatically, 2) creates a project, 3) creates a task, 4) drags it from 'Not Started' to 'In Progress', 5) reloads, 6) asserts the task is in the In Progress column. Use semantic queries (getByRole/getByLabel) per .cursor/skills/write-e2e-test/SKILL.md. If env credentials aren't present, test.skip() the suite — don't fail.
acceptance_criteria: e2e/orat.spec.ts exists, runs against a Supabase project (or test.skip's cleanly when env is absent), and exercises the full create-project → create-task → drag flow.

title: Build e2e/helpers/auth.ts: programmatic Supabase login → cookies
description: The .cursor/skills/write-e2e-test/SKILL.md documents two auth strategies but the helper at e2e/helpers/auth.ts doesn't exist yet. Build it. Read .cursor/skills/write-e2e-test/SKILL.md first and follow whichever strategy it endorses. The helper should expose a function like loginAs(page, { email, password }) that performs a Supabase signInWithPassword via the @supabase/supabase-js client and seeds the resulting auth cookies on the Playwright BrowserContext, so tests can goto('/orat') without going through the login form. Document required env (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, plus a TEST_USER_EMAIL/TEST_USER_PASSWORD for fixtures).
acceptance_criteria: e2e/helpers/auth.ts exists and exports a login helper used by at least one new spec (see follow-up issue). Helper handles cookie names that match @supabase/ssr conventions (sb-<ref>-auth-token). README/inline comment explains required env vars.

title: Add unit tests for task-utils.ts and inject 'today' instead of capturing at module load
description: src/app/orat/utils/task-utils.ts has 4 pure functions used by every view (Kanban, List, Gantt, DashboardMetrics) and currently has zero tests. The TODAY constant is captured at module load — a known timezone footgun. Refactor isOverdue and getEffectiveStatus to take an optional 'today' parameter (default: today's date in YYYY-MM-DD). Add a colocated src/app/orat/utils/task-utils.test.ts covering: isOverdue (overdue, due today, future, complete-and-overdue), getEffectiveStatus (returns 'Complete' regardless of date, returns 'Overdue' when past-due and not complete, otherwise returns task.status), formatDate (en-US format, handles timezones), getStatusBadgeVariant (every TaskStatus mapped). Update the 2 call sites (KanbanView, ListView, GanttView, DashboardMetrics, etc — search for getEffectiveStatus/isOverdue importers) only if they need to pass 'today'; default-arg keeps them working. Do NOT change the public type/return shape.
acceptance_criteria: task-utils.test.ts has >=10 cases, all pass. isOverdue(task, today?) and getEffectiveStatus(task, today?) accept an optional today (YYYY-MM-DD). All existing call sites still build. npm run test:run is green.

title: Forgot / reset password flow
description: There is no password recovery route in the app today. Add a 'Forgot password?' link on /login that opens a request page (calls supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail), an /auth/callback path that exchanges the recovery code, and a /reset-password page where the user sets a new password (with confirmation, length policy, and strength feedback consistent with #2/#3/#8). The request screen must not reveal whether an email is registered (consistent with #4).
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/reset-forgotten-password.feature.

title: Resend verification email from verify-email page
description: The /signup/verify-email page currently tells users to 'try signing up again' if they did not receive the email, which reruns supabase.auth.signUp and risks the enumeration error from #4. Replace with a 'Resend verification email' button that calls supabase.auth.resend({ type: 'signup', email }), with a cooldown to prevent abuse and a friendly message for already-verified accounts. Depends on #1 (email confirmations actually enabled).
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/resend-verification-email.feature.

title: Password strength feedback on sign up
description: Sign up only validates password length. Add a live strength indicator (Weak/Medium/Strong) and visible rules (length, character variety) so users can choose strong passwords without trial and error. Keep server-side enforcement as the source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/signup-password-strength-feedback.feature.

title: Show/hide password toggle on auth forms
description: Password fields on /signup, /login, and the future password-reset form should have an eye-icon toggle that lets users reveal what they typed without losing the value. Reduces typo-induced lockouts, especially before #2 (Confirm password) ships.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/password-show-hide-toggle.feature.

title: Add autoComplete attributes to auth forms
description: Email and password inputs on /signup and /login lack autoComplete attributes, so password managers do not reliably offer autofill or save prompts. Add autoComplete='email', autoComplete='new-password' (signup), and autoComplete='current-password' (login). Apply the same pattern to the future password-reset form.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/auth-forms-support-password-managers.feature.

title: Normalize email on sign up and log in
description: Email is sent to Supabase exactly as typed. 'Foo@Example.com' and 'foo@example.com' should be treated as the same account. Trim and lowercase the email before calling supabase.auth.signUp / signInWithPassword so users cannot accidentally create duplicate accounts or fail to log in due to capitalisation.
acceptance_criteria: See e2e/signup-normalize-email.feature.

title: P2: Mobile site-walk experience
description: Touch-first UI for site walks: large tap targets, photo capture, mobile-friendly status change, offline draft. Today's drag/drop + fixed-height Gantt are hostile on phones.

title: P2: Construction primitives (RFIs, submittals, change orders)
description: First-class RFI and submittal entities (new tables, RLS, UI). Often a separate epic each. Punch list, daily reports, inspections optional follow-ons.

title: P2: Real activity log to replace history JSON
description: Today task.history is a client-appended JSON array with author='Current User'. Move to server-side audit log table with real auth.uid() attribution; surface in TaskDialog.

title: P2: Gantt zoom + drag-to-reschedule + full horizon
description: Today fixed to plus/minus 7 days with no zoom. Add zoom-out (week/month/quarter), pan, and drag bar edges to reschedule (with optimistic update + server save).

title: P2: Weekly status report export per client
description: One-click PDF or email digest summarising open/overdue/upcoming tasks scoped to a client. Owners' reps deliver these manually today.

title: P2: Attachments on tasks (photos, PDFs, drawings)
description: Supabase Storage bucket per org. New orat_task_attachments table with RLS. Upload/preview/delete UI in TaskDialog. Image/PDF preview.

title: P2: Task dependencies and blockers
description: Add a 'blocks/blocked by' relation between tasks. Visual dependency lines on Gantt. Auto-flag overdue cascades. Filter for blocked tasks.

title: P2: Multi-org per user (workspace switcher)
description: Relax unique(user_id) constraint on organization_members. Add a workspace switcher in the chrome. Adjust onboarding + middleware accordingly.

title: P2: Cross-org collaborators on a project
description: Allow inviting a user from a different organization to collaborate on a single project. Builds on True external collaborator model + multi-org.

title: Stand up supabase start in CI for an integration tier (RLS gate)
description: AGENTS.md says 'RLS is the source of truth for security' but nothing actually verifies that claim today. Wire 'supabase start' (local stack) into .github/workflows/ci.yml as a new integration job. Apply migrations (supabase db reset / supabase db push), seed two orgs and two users (a small SQL fixture or TS seed script), then run a small Vitest integration suite that uses the real Supabase JS client (not the recorder fake) to assert: (a) USER_A in ORG_A sees ORG_A projects, (b) USER_B in ORG_B sees ZERO ORG_A projects when calling getProjectsForOrganization(ORG_A), (c) cross-org createTask is rejected. Place tests under src/app/orat/lib/__integration__/ or e2e-rls/ — and exclude them from the normal vitest run by file pattern so unit tests stay fast. Replace the placeholder NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL with the local stack's URL only in this job.
acceptance_criteria: CI integration job spins up supabase start, applies migrations, seeds 2 orgs/users, runs integration tests that prove RLS rejects cross-org reads/writes. Unit suite remains unaffected and fast.

title: Add Vitest coverage reporting (vitest --coverage) with thresholds for orat/lib + orat/utils
description: Add @vitest/coverage-v8 to devDeps. Configure coverage in vitest.config.ts: provider 'v8', reporter ['text','html','lcov'], include only src/**, exclude .next/, types files, and *.test.*. Add a 'test:coverage' npm script. Add per-directory thresholds so PRs can't drop coverage on src/app/orat/lib/** and src/app/orat/utils/** below the level achieved after the other priority issues land (set conservatively: e.g. lines 70%/branches 60% — pick numbers that the suite actually meets at the time of landing). Wire it into .github/workflows/ci.yml (a new step or existing test step) so PRs fail when coverage regresses.
acceptance_criteria: npm run test:coverage produces v8 coverage report. vitest.config.ts has coverage thresholds for orat/lib + orat/utils. CI runs coverage and fails when below thresholds.

title: Delete dead code: src/app/orat/components/TaskOwnershipFilter.tsx
description: Per the audit (TEST_COVERAGE_AUDIT.md §3 final note), TaskOwnershipFilter.tsx has no importers. Verify with grep, then delete. Keep this in the same pass as the test cleanup so the diff stays self-contained.
acceptance_criteria: src/app/orat/components/TaskOwnershipFilter.tsx is removed. ripgrep shows zero importers before deletion. npm run build passes.
